requirements: Maintain structural integrity under spring deployment impulse (<100g shock)
Ensure electrical continuity break via mechanical kill switch arm upon separation
Provide precise alignment for P-POD/ISIPOD pusher contact interface

risk.desc: Contact surface deformation of pusher_contact_boss under high impulse spring load.
risk.mit: Hardened steel (A2) insert press-fit into Al 7075 boss to prevent galling/pitting.

risk.desc: Kill_switch_arm pivot friction due to thermal expansion mismatch in vacuum.
risk.mit: Dry-film lubricant (MoS2) coating on revolute bearing surfaces and pin interfaces.

risk.desc: Harness_routing_channel edge sharpness causing wire insulation abrasion during vibration.
risk.mit: Mandatory 1.5mm radius chamfer on all channel edges and service ports.

risk.desc: Stress concentration at stiffening_rib_structure/base junction during separation impulse.
risk.mit: Implement 2.0mm fillets at all rib-to-plate intersections to smooth load path transitions.

schema_v6_blueprint._part_spec: P-POD or ISIPOD separation interface plate, spring pusher contact, kill switch arm

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Provides mechanical separation interface between the launch vehicle (P-POD/ISIPOD) and the 3U CubeSat, managing spring deployment loads and electrical kill-switch telemetry.

physics_paths.path: Spring pusher force -> Pusher contact pad -> Separation Spring Plate (p8) -> Primary Structure Rails (p1)
physics_paths.design_implication: Requires high stiffness in the longitudinal axis and reinforced mounting bosses to prevent buckling during deployment.

physics_paths.path: Kill switch arm contact -> Electrical harness passthrough -> Avionics bus
physics_paths.design_implication: Requires dedicated cable routing channels and non-conductive isolation zones near the kill switch pivot.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install p8 after primary frame (p1) is validated.
Pusher contact must be clear of pod floor before integration.
Route harness through p8 before final enclosure.

internal_features.function: Main load-bearing plate for deployment forces.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Al 7075 CNC milled slab with stiffening ribs.

internal_features.function: Point of contact for the deployment spring.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Cylindrical boss with hardened surface coating.

internal_features.geometry_hint: Revolute hinge with pivot pin and contact pad.

internal_features.geometry_hint: Four corner bosses with threaded inserts.

internal_features.geometry_hint: Recessed channel with chamfered edges.

internal_features.function: Prevents plate deformation under spring load.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Patterned ribs milled into underside.

integration_opportunities: merge mounts, routing channels, ribs, labels, and service datums into the printed subsystem where inspection still remains possible

manufacturing_strategy.process_basis: 5-axis CNC milling from Al 7075 billet to ensure grain flow alignment for load paths.
manufacturing_strategy.datum_strategy: Primary datum is the plate's central axis [0,0,z] and two parallel side faces (X/Y planes).
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance_strategy: H7 fit on pivot pin holes; ±0.05mm on pusher contact center position.
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Flat face down for milling stability; vertical orientation preferred for secondary anodizing.
manufacturing_strategy.inspection_strategy: CMM verification of boss parallelism and hole perpendicularity.

verification_focus: Deployment force vector alignment
Kill-switch contact continuity under vibration
Structural margin against spring deployment impulse

generated_parts.label: Primary Structure (3U)

requirements: Maintain structural stiffness for 3U CubeSat launch loads (approx. 8G axial)
Provide CDS-compliant rail guide grooves on exterior faces for deployment
Support PC/104 avionics stack via standardized standoff spacing
Enable harness routing through internal side channels

verify.value: CNC machining orientation optimized for rails and standoffs
±0.05mm for CDS grooves via precision milling

risk.desc: Thermal expansion mismatch between Al 7075 frame and FR4 PCBs causing stress on PC/104 standoffs.
risk.mit: Use slotted mounting holes in PCB stack to allow lateral thermal movement.

risk.desc: Galvanic corrosion at interface between Al 7075 and stainless steel fasteners/inserts.
risk.mit: Apply chromate conversion coating or anodization to all aluminum surfaces.

risk.desc: Vibration-induced loosening of M3 standoffs during launch phase.
risk.mit: Use secondary locking via Loctite 242 or spring washers on all structural fasteners.

risk.desc: Harness abrasion within the management channel due to high-frequency vibration.
risk.mit: Line harness channels with compliant edge protection or grommets.

blueprint._part_spec: 3U CubeSat primary frame, Al 7075, PC/104 standoffs, deployable rail guide grooves (CDS spec)

physics_paths.path: Launch vibration -> corner posts -> longitudinal side rails -> separation interface plate
physics_paths.design_implication: High stiffness required in Z-axis; use of Al 7075 extrusions/machined profiles for primary load paths.

physics_paths.path: Internal avionics heat -> PC/104 standoffs (conduction) -> side rails (radiation/convection)
physics_paths.design_implication: Thermal straps or conductive standoffs required to bridge electronics to the chassis.

physics_paths.path: Internal harness routing through dedicated cable channels in frame members
physics_paths.design_implication: Integration of hollowed-out side rails for wire management and EMI shielding.

manufacturing_strategy.process_basis: CNC milling of Al 7075-T6 extrusions for rails; precision drilling/tapping for standoffs.
manufacturing_strategy.datum_strategy: Bottom separation plate (p8) acts as primary datum [X,Y]; corner posts establish Z alignment via 3-2-1 method.
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance_strategy: CDS rail grooves require ±0.05mm tolerance; PCB standoff holes M3 +H7/g6.
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Vertical machining orientation for rails to minimize tool deflection on long extrusions.
manufacturing_strategy.inspection_strategy: Coordinate Measuring Machine (CMM) check of corner squareness and rail parallelism.

verification_focus: Check structural stiffness against launch vibration profile
Verify CDS deployment clearance in all 4 axes
Validate thermal conductivity path from internal electronics to frame rails

generated_parts.label: Solar Panel × 4 (deploy)
generated_parts.spec: Deployable solar panel wing, 3U size, spring-loaded hinge, burn-wire release, deployment damper

requirements: Deployable solar array wings providing electrical power via PV cells
Spring-loaded hinge mechanism for 90-degree deployment axis
Burn-wire release system for stowed position retention
Deployment damper to mitigate structural shock during end-of-travel

verify.value: All hinge cavities and channels oriented for self-supporting geometry.
±0.02mm on hinge pin diameter via precision boring.

risk.desc: Burn-wire thermal soak could damage CFRP substrate resin near release point.
risk.mit: Incorporate localized Al 7075 heat sink boss around the burn_wire_note zone.

risk.desc: Harness pinching during high-velocity deployment from stowed to deployed state.
risk.mit: Define minimum bend radius in harness_channel geometry and add guide ribs.

risk.desc: Deployment shock exceeding structural limits of solar cell interconnects.
risk.mit: Optimize deployment_damper housing damping coefficient via viscous fluid fill.

blueprint._part_spec: Deployable solar panel wing, 3U size, spring-loaded hinge, burn-wire release, deployment damper

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Deployable solar array wings providing electrical power via photovoltaic cells, utilizing spring-loaded hinges and burn-wire release mechanisms for deployment from stowed 3U configuration.

physics_paths.path: Solar cell tension/vibration -> CFRP substrate -> hinge pin -> primary frame (p1)
physics_paths.design_implication: Requires high-stiffness CFRP stringers and robust clevis pins at the hinge axis.

physics_paths.path: Photovoltaic cells -> harness channel in substrate -> connector mate to p2 bus
physics_paths.design_implication: Integrated routing channels required within panel thickness to prevent wire pinching during deployment.

physics_paths.path: Solar cell heat absorption -> CFRP skin conduction -> radiator surface (exterior)
physics_paths.design_implication: Substrate must include thermal interface material (TIM) zones and controlled emissivity surfaces.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install after p1 frame rails are verified for parallelism.
Connect last before final enclosure closure.
Assemble hinge assembly prior to panel skin attachment.

verify.value: All internal pockets and channels utilize chamfered transitions to avoid unsupported overhangs.

risk.desc: Thermal soak from burn-wire heater to primary structure (p1) via conduction.

requirements: Provide high-precision 3-axis attitude control via momentum exchange (0.005-0.05 Nms)
Isolate motor vibration from EO payload to minimize jitter
Maintain thermal stability for brushless motor windings and bearing assembly
Ensure EMI shielding of magnetic field within the enclosure

verify.value: All internal pockets and baffle_shield_recess designed with 45deg draft for CNC/Additive hybrid.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion mismatch between Al 7075 housing and CFRP rotor causing bearing preload shift.
risk.mit: Implement thermal isolation ring at the motor_stator_housing/bearing interface.

risk.desc: Vibration from reaction_wheel_dummy may bypass isolation_mount_bosses via rigid electrical harness.
risk.mit: Utilize flexible braided shielding in the harness_channel to decouple mechanical paths.

risk.desc: Stray light ingress through baffle_shield_recess affecting star tracker sensitivity.
risk.mit: Apply Vantablack-type coating to the internal conical geometry of the recess.

risk.desc: High centrifugal loads on CFRP rotor edges during rapid momentum changes.
risk.mit: Incorporate a metallic rim retention ring integrated into the reaction_wheel_dummy design.

risk.desc: Harness pinching in harness_channel during CubeSat deployment/vibration.
risk.mit: Add chamfered edges to all channel entries and specify cable tie points on p1 frame.

physics_paths.path: Motor windings heat -> Housing conduction -> Thermal straps/Radiator path to p6
physics_paths.design_implication: Integrated thermal conductive paths in Al 7075 housing and dedicated vent ports for convection-free vacuum operation.

physics_paths.path: Avionics (p1) -> Harness through p4 chassis -> Motor windings/Hall sensors

Provide high-stiffness mounting for star tracker optical bench to prevent jitter amplification
Attenuate stray sunlight via internal baffle shielding and matte coating

verify.value: Internal baffle ribs at 52° from vertical
Vertical orientation of baffle_shield_assembly minimizes internal support contact.

risk.desc: Thermal expansion mismatch between Al 7075 base and CFRP/Invar inserts may induce LOS tilt.
risk.mit: Use graded transition interfaces or localized flexures in the mounting flange design.

risk.desc: Internal baffle surface roughness from LPBF process causing stray light scattering.
risk.mit: Specify chemical vapor smoothing or abrasive flow machining for internal cone surfaces.

risk.desc: Harness_passthrough_channel edges may cause cable insulation abrasion during launch vibration.

risk.desc: Residual stress in LPBF baffle_shield_assembly leading to geometric warping of the sensor window plane.

blueprint._part_spec: Star tracker optical bench mount, CTE-matched, baffled sun shield, line-of-sight ±1°

physics_paths.path: Launch vibration/jitter -> Star Tracker Mount Flange -> Primary Structure Rails (p1)
physics_paths.design_implication: Requires high-stiffness ribbing and bolted interface to prevent micro-vibration amplification.

physics_paths.path: Solar radiation/Internal Avionics heat -> Baffle Shield -> Optical Bench (Radiative isolation)

manufacturing_strategy.process_basis: CNC milling for Al 7075 baseplate; additive manufacturing (DMLS) possible for complex baffle geometry.
manufacturing_strategy.datum_strategy: Primary datum: Baseplate flatness [X,Y]; Secondary datum: Side-wall parallelism to Z-axis.
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance_strategy: Critical fit on lens mounts (+/- 0.01mm); non-critical mounting holes +/- 0.1mm.
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Baseplate flat on machine bed; baffle rings oriented vertically for optimal surface finish.
manufacturing_strategy.inspection_strategy: CMM verification of boresight alignment and flatness; thermal vacuum testing of sensor pocket.

verification_focus: Boresight error between lens center and focal plane datum
Thermal stability (drift < 50um over operating range)
Harness bend radius compliance in raceway

generated_parts.label: Cold Gas Thruster × 4
generated_parts.spec: Cold gas propulsion thruster bracket, 4 thrusters cross-pattern, 50mN class, plumbing manifold

requirements: Mount 4x cold gas thrusters in a cross-pattern (X, Y axes)
Distribute propellant via internal manifold channels to nozzle inlets
Provide thermal isolation between nozzles and primary CubeSat structure
Maintain structural stiffness for launch vibration loads

verify.value: Manifold internal channels require sacrificial support or optimized teardrop profile.
±0.05mm required for O-ring seal lands; post-process machining recommended.

risk.desc: Internal manifold roughness causing turbulent flow and pressure drop in 50mN thrusters.
risk.mit: Specify post-process abrasive flow machining (AFM) for internal channels.

risk.desc: Thermal conduction through Al/Ti hybrid interface causing heat soak to p1 structure.
risk.mit: Incorporate ceramic G10 spacers or thermal breaks at the primary mounting datum.

risk.desc: Residual stress in LPBF part causing warping of the cross-pattern mount plane.
risk.mit: Implement stress relief heat treatment cycle prior to removal from build plate.

blueprint._part_spec: Cold gas propulsion thruster bracket, 4 thrusters cross-pattern, 50mN class, plumbing manifold

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Structural mounting for 4x cold gas thrusters in cross-pattern and central manifold distribution to regulate propellant flow from the main tank.

physics_paths.path: Thruster thrust vector -> nozzle flange -> bracket ribbing -> primary structure (p1) mounting rails
physics_paths.design_implication: High-stiffness Al 7075 ribs required between thruster mounts to prevent structural resonance.

physics_paths.path: Propellant tank -> manifold inlet -> internal channels -> nozzle injectors
physics_paths.design_implication: Internal fluidic paths must be machined/drilled and capped with o-ring sealed plugs to prevent leakage in vacuum.

physics_paths.path: Nozzle heat soak -> bracket conduction path -> primary structure (p1) thermal straps
physics_paths.design_implication: Thermal isolation via low-conductivity spacers or ceramic washers at thruster interfaces to protect avionics.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install bracket before thrusters and plumbing lines.
adjacent_interfaces.required_feature: M3 mounting holes on datum face

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Connect manifold to tank before final thruster installation.
Last step: screw thruster units into bracket.

internal_features.name: thruster_bracket_base
internal_features.function: Main structural plate for subsystem mounting to p1.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Shell/Plate 3mm thick with chamfered edges.

internal_features.name: cross_pattern_mounts
internal_features.function: Locating and fastening points for the 4 thrusters.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Four M4 tapped bosses with integrated ribs.

internal_features.name: plumbing_manifold_body
internal_features.function: Distributes gas to individual thruster lines.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Internal cylindrical channels and central junction pocket.

internal_features.name: harness_channel
internal_features.function: Protects electrical lines for thruster valves/sensors.
internal_features.geometry_hint: U-shaped pocket along the bracket edge.

internal_features.name: thermal_isolation_bosses
internal_features.function: Prevents heat transfer from nozzle to frame.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Small raised cylindrical standoffs with recessed faces for washers.

internal_features.name: inspection_port
internal_features.function: Allows visual/sensor check of manifold seals.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Small 5mm diameter clearance hole in base plate.

integration_opportunities: merge mounts, routing channels, ribs, labels, and service datums into the printed subsystem where inspection still remains possible

manufacturing_strategy.process_basis: CNC Milling (5-axis) for manifold channels and mounting bosses in Al 7075.
manufacturing_strategy.datum_strategy: Primary datum: Bottom face [z=0] relative to p1 rail. Secondary datum: Centerline X/Y axes through cross pattern.
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance_strategy: H7 fit on thruster interface bores; +/- 0.02mm for manifold seal lands.
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Vertical orientation (Z-up) during milling to minimize tool reach into deep channels.
manufacturing_strategy.inspection_strategy: Pressure decay test of internal manifold and CMM check of mounting hole parallelism.

verification_focus: Manifold leak rate under 5 bar proof pressure
Alignment accuracy of the four thruster axes (concentricity)
Structural stiffness/eigenfrequency vs launch profile requirements

generated_parts.label: Separation Spring Plate
generated_parts.spec: P-POD or ISIPOD separation interface plate, spring pusher contact, kill switch arm

requirements: Maintain structural integrity under spring deployment impulse (<100g shock)
Ensure electrical continuity break via mechanical kill switch arm upon separation
Provide precise alignment for P-POD/ISIPOD pusher contact interface

risk.desc: Contact surface deformation of pusher_contact_boss under high impulse spring load.
risk.mit: Hardened steel (A2) insert press-fit into Al 7075 boss to prevent galling/pitting.

risk.desc: Kill_switch_arm pivot friction due to thermal expansion mismatch in vacuum.
risk.mit: Dry-film lubricant (MoS2) coating on revolute bearing surfaces and pin interfaces.

risk.desc: Harness_routing_channel edge sharpness causing wire insulation abrasion during vibration.
risk.mit: Mandatory 1.5mm radius chamfer on all channel edges and service ports.

risk.desc: Stress concentration at stiffening_rib_structure/base junction during separation impulse.
risk.mit: Implement 2.0mm fillets at all rib-to-plate intersections to smooth load path transitions.

blueprint._part_spec: P-POD or ISIPOD separation interface plate, spring pusher contact, kill switch arm

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Provides mechanical separation interface between the launch vehicle (P-POD/ISIPOD) and the 3U CubeSat, managing spring deployment loads and electrical kill-switch telemetry.

physics_paths.path: Spring pusher force -> Pusher contact pad -> Separation Spring Plate (p8) -> Primary Structure Rails (p1)
physics_paths.design_implication: Requires high stiffness in the longitudinal axis and reinforced mounting bosses to prevent buckling during deployment.

physics_paths.path: Kill switch arm contact -> Electrical harness passthrough -> Avionics bus
physics_paths.design_implication: Requires dedicated cable routing channels and non-conductive isolation zones near the kill switch pivot.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install p8 after primary frame (p1) is validated.
Pusher contact must be clear of pod floor before integration.
Route harness through p8 before final enclosure.

internal_features.name: separation_spring_plate
internal_features.function: Main load-bearing plate for deployment forces.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Al 7075 CNC milled slab with stiffening ribs.

internal_features.name: pusher_contact
internal_features.function: Point of contact for the deployment spring.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Cylindrical boss with hardened surface coating.

internal_features.name: kill_switch_arm
internal_features.function: Mechanical arm to break electrical circuit upon deployment.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Revolute hinge with pivot pin and contact pad.

internal_features.name: mounting_bosses
internal_features.function: Secure attachment to CubeSat rails.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Four corner bosses with threaded inserts.

internal_features.name: wire_routing_channel
internal_features.function: Protects deployment wires from shear forces.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Recessed channel with chamfered edges.

internal_features.name: stiffening_ribs
internal_features.function: Prevents plate deformation under spring load.
internal_features.geometry_hint: Patterned ribs milled into underside.

integration_opportunities: merge mounts, routing channels, ribs, labels, and service datums into the printed subsystem where inspection still remains possible

manufacturing_strategy.process_basis: 5-axis CNC milling from Al 7075 billet to ensure grain flow alignment for load paths.
manufacturing_strategy.datum_strategy: Primary datum is the plate's central axis [0,0,z] and two parallel side faces (X/Y planes).
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance_strategy: H7 fit on pivot pin holes; ±0.05mm on pusher contact center position.
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Flat face down for milling stability; vertical orientation preferred for secondary anodizing.
manufacturing_strategy.inspection_strategy: CMM verification of boss parallelism and hole perpendicularity.

verification_focus: Deployment force vector alignment
Kill-switch contact continuity under vibration
Structural margin against spring deployment impulse
